title: WA CFS Q1 2024: 18.98% Decline in Credit Generation
content:
Key Takeaways

  • Credit generation fell by 18.98% in Q1'24, totaling 462,215 credits.
  • Deficits surged to 246,767, a 25.92% increase from Q4'23, driven by significant rises in ethanol, gasoline, and diesel deficits.
  • Renewable diesel credits increased by 59.96%, while bio-CNG credits jumped 93.98%, despite bio-CNG comprising only 2.58% of the total credit pool.
  • Diesel and gasoline volumes declined by 3.68% and 6.87%, respectively, while renewable diesel volume rose by 20.87%.
author: Mansift Kaur
